revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:飛騨総社]]
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hida Sōja | image = File:Hida Sōja Shrine Haiden.jpg | location = 2-114 Kanda-chō, Takayama, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.148189|N|137.254172|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Ōyatsuhashi no Mikoto; Eight Shrines from the {{ill|Engishiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}; Ten Shrines listed in national historical records|Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}; {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119929592}} | festivals = {{nihongo|May 4|5月4日}} and {{nihongo|May 5|5月5日}} | Rituals = | map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|label=Hida Sōja|coordinates={{Coord2|36|8|53.4|N|137|15|15|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|8|53.4|N|137|15|15|E}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Takayama|qid=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} }}
[[File:Hida Sōja Shrine Torii on the street.jpg|thumb|250px|Second torii gate]] [[File:Hida Sōja Shrine Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|First torii gate]]
'''Hida Sōja''' (飛騨総社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|qid=Q622594|lt=Takayama|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|qid=Q1107129|lt=Sōja shrine|1=Sōja shrine}}, or central shrine, of {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|qid=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as enshrining eight deities (八座) and in the national histories for ten shrines (十座).
The shrine holds its annual festival on {{ill|May 4|en|5月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}} and {{ill|May 5|en|5月5日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 5|qq=}}, featuring a thousand-person procession and a unique parent-child lion dance involving acrobatics by 22 performers.
== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Main Hall (Honden) === * Ōyatsukihime no Mikoto (大八椅命) ''' A descendant of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|Amenohoakari|qid=Q10940411|qid=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|1=Amenohoakari}}, the first Hida kokuzō (regional governor).
=== Side Halls (Engishiki Jinmyōchō Deities) === The shrine enshrines eight deities listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō: * Minashi-no-Kami (水無神) – {{ill|Minashi Shrine|en|Minashi Shrine|qid=Q704865|lt=Minashi Shrine|1=Minashi Shrine}}, deity of {{ill|Mount Kurai|en|Mount Kurai|qid=Q6921728|lt=Mount Kurai|1=Mount Kurai}}. * Tsukimoto-no-Kami (槻本神) – {{ill|Tsukimoto Shrine|ja|槻本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukimoto Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. * Ena-no-Kami (荏名神) – {{ill|Ena Shrine|ja|荏名神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|1=Takamimusubi}}. * Ōtsu-no-Kami (大津神) – {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|ja|大津神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|qq=}}, deities {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Takeninukawawake|ja|武渟川別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeninukawawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}}. * Araki-no-Kami (荒城神) – {{ill|Araki Shrine|ja|荒城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araki Shrine|1=Araki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11618425}}, Ōaraki-no-Mikoto (大荒木之命). * Takata-no-Kami (高田神) – {{ill|Takata Shrine (Hida)|ja|高田神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takata Shrine (Hida)|qq=}}, Takami-no-Mikoto (高魂命). * Atayuta-no-Kami (阿多由太神) – {{ill|Atayuta Shrine|ja|阿多由太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atayuta Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. * Kurihara-no-Kami (栗原神) – {{ill|Kurihara Shrine (Takayama)|ja|栗原神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurihara Shrine (Takayama)|qq=}}, {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|qq=|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}.
=== Side Halls (Deities Listed in National Histories) === The shrine also enshrines ten deities noted in Japan's national histories: * {{ill|Toshigami|en|Toshigami|qid=Q3028663|lt=Toshigami|1=Toshigami}} * Hashiribuchi-no-Kami (走淵神) * Shitennō-no-Kami (四天王神) * Yuhataishi-no-Kami (遊幡石神) * Watarise-no-Kami (渡瀬神) * Dōgo-no-Kami (道後神) * Keta-Wakamiya-no-Kami (気多若宮神) * Honmomikokutsu-no-Kami (本母国津神) * Kenwo-no-Kami (剣緒神) * Kamo-Wakamiya-no-Kami (加茂若宮神)
=== Merged Deities === * {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} * {{ill|Kikurihime|en|Kikurihime|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime|1=Kikurihime}}
== History ==
The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Jōhei|en|Jōhei|qid=Q1324874|lt=Shōhei era|1=Jōhei}} (931–938). Its first shrine building was constructed in 1191 ({{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|qid=Q1194415|lt=Bunji|1=Bunji (era)}} 2). Despite flourishing in its early years, it fell into decline during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, major renovations were carried out by {{ill|Kanamori Shigeyori|ja|金森重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamori Shigeyori|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei era|1=Kan'ei}}. However, the shrine again declined during the {{ill|Great Tenmei famine|en|Great Tenmei famine|qid=Q9030108|lt=Great Tenmei famine|1=Great Tenmei famine}} (1781), reducing its grounds.
In 1820 ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 3), following the publication of "Hida Sōja Kō" (飛騨総社考) by {{ill|Tanaka Ōhide|en|Tanaka Ōhide|qid=Q6536652|lt=Tanaka Ōhide|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}, a student of {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|qid=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}}, the shrine was restored and its grounds expanded. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, major construction was carried out from 1887 to 1890, reorienting the shrine eastward and formalizing the a11roach.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Gifu Prefecture Designated Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Takayama Matsuri Yatai|ja|高山祭屋台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Matsuri Yatai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/tkayamasi/takayamanmaturiyatai.html |title=Takayama Matsuri Yatai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}}</ref>
== location == * Address: Kanda-cho 2-114, Takayama City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|qid=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|qid=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
